heading: Assertive community treatment grant program
label: 290bb–44 Assertive community treatment grant program

children.text: to establish assertive community treatment programs for adults with a serious mental illness; or
to maintain or expand such programs.

children.heading: Eligible entities
children.label: b Eligible entities
children.text: To be eligible to receive a grant under this section, an entity shall be a State, political subdivision of a State, Indian Tribe or Tribal organization (as such terms are defined in ), mental health system, health care facility, or any other entity the Assistant Secretary deems appropriate.section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Special consideration
children.label: c Special consideration
children.text: In selecting among applicants for a grant under this section, the Assistant Secretary may give special consideration to the potential of the applicant’s program to reduce hospitalization, homelessness, and involvement with the criminal justice system while improving the health and social outcomes of the patient.

children.label: A
children.text: any cost savings and public health outcomes such as mortality, suicide, substance use disorders, hospitalization, and use of services;

children.label: B
children.text: rates of involvement with the criminal justice system of patients;

children.label: C
children.text: rates of homelessness among patients; and

children.label: D
children.text: patient and family satisfaction with program participation; and

provide appropriate information, training, and technical assistance to grant recipients under this section to help such recipients to establish, maintain, or expand their assertive community treatment programs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: To carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated $9,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2023 through 2027.

children.heading: Use of certain funds
children.label: 2 Use of certain funds
children.text: Of the funds appropriated to carry out this section in any fiscal year, not more than 5 percent shall be available to the Assistant Secretary for carrying out subsection (d).
